data.content: Now I need to download debug symbols for these .NET libraries and symbolicate the addresses. Let me check available tools and fetch symbols from Microsoft's symbol server.

arguments.command: which addr2line llvm-symbolizer llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; dpkg -l | grep -i llvm 2>/dev/null | head -5
which dotnet 2>/dev/null && dotnet --version 2>/dev/null; which dotnet-symbol 2>/dev/null

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to symbolicate the .NET runtime frames in this Android tombstone. The key frames are from:

1. `libmonosgen-2.0.so` (BuildId: 1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be) - frames #01, #02, #03, #06
2. `libSystem.Native.so` (BuildId: 7a3b5c9d1e4f6082a3c5d7e9f1b2d4a6c8e0f213) - frames #04, #07


3. `libSystem.Globalization.Native.so` (BuildId: 2f4a6b8c0d2e4f6a8c0e2f4a6b8c0d2e4f6a8c0e) - frame #05

To symbolicate these frames, I'll need the corresponding debug symbols for each library using their build IDs.

Now I'm looking at how to retrieve these symbols from Microsoft's symbol server. For .NET runtime libraries, Microsoft hosts symbol packages that can be fetched using the build ID, though the exact URL format for ELF binaries might differ from the standard Windows format.

The most practical approach would be to use the `dotnet-symbol` tool to automate the download process, or manually fetch the symbols from the server and then use `addr2line` or `llvm-symbolizer` to map the addresses back to source locations. Let me check what tools are available to work with.

arguments.command: which addr2line llvm-symbolizer llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; dpkg -l | grep -i llvm 2>/dev/null | head -5
which dotnet 2>/dev/null && dotnet --version 2>/dev/null; which dotnet-symbol 2>/dev/null

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
cd /tmp/sv-c66b5a5226314ad19763f9800b61f080 && mkdir -p symbols && cd symbols

# Download symbols for libmonosgen-2.0.so (BuildId: 1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be)
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
FILENAME="libmonosgen-2.0.so"
curl -sL -o "${FILENAME}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}" && echo "Downloaded ${FILENAME} ($(wc -c < ${FILENAME}) bytes)" || echo "Failed to download ${FILENAME}"

# Also try .dbg file
curl -sL -o "${FILENAME}.dbg" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}.dbg/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}.dbg" && echo "Downloaded ${FILENAME}.dbg ($(wc -c < ${FILENAME}.dbg}) bytes)" || echo "Failed to download ${FILENAME}.dbg"
arguments.description: Download monosgen symbols from MS symbol server

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Download monosgen symbols from MS symbol server

data.reasoningText:
We have `dotnet-symbol` and `addr2line` available. Let me use `dotnet-symbol` to download the symbols for these libraries using their build IDs.

Actually, `dotnet-symbol` works with dump files or directly with ELF build IDs. Let me try fetching the symbols from the symbol server directly.

The approach:
1. Use the Microsoft symbol server to download debug symbols using the build IDs
2. Use addr2line to symbolicate the addresses

The build IDs and libraries:
- libmonosgen-2.0.so: 1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be (frames at offsets: 0x222098, 0x222234, 0x19bac0, 0x19bc14)


- libSystem.Native.so: 7a3b5c9d1e4f6082a3c5d7e9f1b2d4a6c8e0f213 (frames at offsets: 0x8f14, 0x12a48)
- libSystem.Globalization.Native.so: 2f4a6b8c0d2e4f6a8c0e2f4a6b8c0d2e4f6a8c0e (frame at offset: 0x3b20)

Now I need to construct the Microsoft symbol server URL for ELF files to download these symbols.

I could use the `dotnet-symbol` tool to handle this automatically, but since we only have the tombstone data without the actual .so files, let me try downloading directly from the symbol server using the proper URL format for ELF binaries and their debug symbols.

arguments.command:
cd /tmp/sv-c66b5a5226314ad19763f9800b61f080 && mkdir -p symbols && cd symbols

# Download symbols for libmonosgen-2.0.so (BuildId: 1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be)
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
FILENAME="libmonosgen-2.0.so"
curl -sL -o "${FILENAME}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}" && echo "Downloaded ${FILENAME} ($(wc -c < ${FILENAME}) bytes)" || echo "Failed to download ${FILENAME}"

# Also try .dbg file
curl -sL -o "${FILENAME}.dbg" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}.dbg/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}.dbg" && echo "Downloaded ${FILENAME}.dbg ($(wc -c < ${FILENAME}.dbg}) bytes)" || echo "Failed to download ${FILENAME}.dbg"
